reasoning: The baseline chance for a weekly series increasing is around 50%. The FRED primary credit series has experienced a pronounced jump to 5216.0 for the week ending March 25, 2026, which is higher than the roughly 4400-4800 cluster seen in February and early March. Similar sudden jumps in recent history (e.g. Feb 18 to Feb 25, or over Dec 2025) have frequently been followed by mean reversion the following week. Additionally, quarter-end window dressing may encourage banks to clear their discount window borrowing before March 31 reporting, leading to a possible lower reading on April 1. These factors combine to give a higher probability of the level decreasing. Giving credit to the FRED automated combined estimate for a similar drop, I adjust slightly below their estimate.
